extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r4, whose condition is the conjunction of 10 inputs including heart_failure. rec_drug_b is set true only by r3, whose condition is heart_failure. Because r4's condition entails heart_failure, any input satisfying r4 also satisfies r3. The two rules write to different outputs (a vs b), so priority/order conflict resolution never suppresses either one. Thus when all 10 of r4's conjuncts are true, r4 sets rec_drug_a=true AND r3 sets rec_drug_b=true simultaneously, making (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b) true and violating the mutual-exclusion property. Concrete counterexample: all 10 conjuncts (heart_failure, ckd_dialysis, polypharmacy, hyperkalemia, renal_impairment, qt_prolongation, g6pd_deficiency, hypertension, bradycardia, prior_gi_bleed) = true; asthma and seizure_history are irrelevant to a/b and set false. This yields rec_drug_a=true, rec_drug_b=true. File analyzed: /Users/sanjaybasu/clinical-formal-verification/experiments/runs/_judge_prompts_blind/item-76be7f499d35.txt
